name: Chameleon API
what_it_does: Provides a REST API for managing Chameleon in-app product tours, tooltips, surveys, and user targeting rules, enabling agents to programmatically control user guidance experiences and retrieve completion data.
use_cases: Create or update tour and tooltip content programmatically from a CMS or localization pipeline without using the Chameleon visual editor
Retrieve survey responses (microsurveys, NPS) to feed customer feedback into product management or CRM workflows
Update user profile properties via the API so tour targeting logic reflects current plan, role, or feature access
Track tour completion and dismissal events via webhooks to trigger downstream onboarding or success workflows
Publish or unpublish tours programmatically as part of a feature flag or release management pipeline
not_for: Rendering tours in the browser — the JavaScript snippet is required for all in-app UI display
Full product analytics with retention, funnel, and cohort analysis; Chameleon is a guidance tool, not an analytics platform
Native mobile app guidance where the web JS snippet is not embeddable
best_when: An agent needs to manage the lifecycle of in-app tours and surveys programmatically, or retrieve user interaction data to drive onboarding automation outside the Chameleon UI.
avoid_when: You need deep product usage analytics beyond tour interaction data; pair Chameleon with a dedicated analytics platform for that use case.

auth.notes: API key passed as X-Account-Secret header. Separate publishable key used for client-side JS SDK. Server-side key provides full account access with no granular scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free plan includes up to 3 tours, 1 survey, and basic analytics. API access available on free tier.
pricing.paid_tiers: Startup (~$279/month), Growth (~$599/month), and Enterprise tiers based on MAUs and feature set.
pricing.notes: Free tier does not require a credit card. API access is available across all tiers, including free.

performance.rate_limits: No publicly documented rate limits; in practice the REST API is low-volume management-oriented and unlikely to be rate-limited under normal agent usage.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API documentation covers core endpoints but is less detailed than the JS SDK documentation. Error responses are JSON with message field but lack error codes for programmatic handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Creating the same tour or survey twice via the API produces duplicates; agents must check existence before creating.
known_agent_gotchas: Tour publishing state (draft vs live) is controlled separately from content; updating a tour's content does not automatically republish it — agents must explicitly set the live state
User profile updates via the API take effect asynchronously and may not immediately affect tour targeting for active sessions
The publishable key and secret key serve different contexts and are easy to confuse; using the secret key client-side is a security vulnerability
Webhook payloads are signed but signature verification documentation is sparse — agents should validate signatures to prevent spoofing
The free tier tour limit (3 tours) is enforced at creation time; API calls to create additional tours will fail with a plan limit error that must be handled gracefully

security_score_components.security_notes: Single secret key with no scope granularity. The distinction between publishable and secret keys is important; leaking the secret key exposes full account management. SOC2 certified.
